data.title: Bybit CEO: I agree with CZ. If the internal system is breached, all withdrawals will be suspended immediately.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bybit CEO Ben Zhou responded to CZ's suggestion to stop Bybit withdrawals on the X platform, saying: "I agree with CZ's point of view. If the hacker attack was through infiltrating our internal system (such as a part of the withdrawal system) or the hot wallet was hacked, we would immediately suspend all withdrawals until the root cause of the problem is found. But in yesterday's incident, it was our ETH cold wallet (we use Safe) that was hacked, which has nothing to do with any of our internal systems. Therefore, I can decisively decide to let all Bybit withdrawals and system functions operate as usual. In the crisis last night, Binance and CZ, as well as many partners and industry leaders, took the initiative to help. We are deeply grateful for this and feel extremely warm for the support we have received. This incident is a huge blow to Bybit, but the entire industry has shown the power of unity. I believe that from now on, everything will only move in a better direction."

data.title: Ethereum Address Holding 20 Million USDT Frozen

data.title: Shenyu: Bybit's $1.5 billion security incident and multiple Safe Owner tampering incidents exposed the current blockchain security issues
Blockchain Security Concerns Highlighted By Cobo CEO
data.description: According to Odaily, Cobo co-founder and CEO recently addressed significant blockchain security issues on platform X. The statement follows a $1.5 billion security incident involving Bybit and several cases of Safe owner modifications. The CEO identified three major pain points in blockchain security: the vulnerability of front-end interfaces and plugins to tampering, which prevents users from verifying signature data; the lack of independence and mandatory review mechanisms in environments holding multi-signature keys; and the limited transaction parsing capabilities of hardware wallets, often resulting in blind signing.

data.title: 1,000 BTC transferred from Bybit to an unknown wallet, equivalent to $96.86 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that according to Whale Alert monitoring, 50 minutes ago, 1,000 BTC were transferred from Bybit to an unknown wallet, equivalent to approximately US$96.86 million.
